currentWave: Persistence completed for every engine-complete function not blocked on business content. Next work requires either live-database verification or an owner decision (pricing is highest-leverage).
workspaceNote: This workspace (LTS Stand Alone Software) is a full copy of Development/Business Website/Website Code/v23, including .git and origin remote. v23 itself was not modified. Standing instruction: everything stays local to this workspace for the entire build -- no push, no merge into a new vN folder or main. Dylan granted bypass permission (2026-07-14) to take care of everything without interruption, with that one hard constraint.

implementedFunctions: 34 functions now have real, tested logic; 21 of them additionally have real (unit-tested, not-yet-live) Postgres persistence -- see REQUIREMENTS_TRACEABILITY.md for the complete function-by-function breakdown

knownRisks: No live Neon database exists yet -- ALL of src/db/ (21 files, 99 tests) is unit-tested against a fake client but has never executed one real query. This has been the top risk since Session 10 and has only grown -- strongly recommend this be resolved before any further persistence work, rather than continuing to add untested-live code indefinitely.
Netlify Blobs' users/sessions/tokens stores (F003/F004) remain unmigrated by design
Existing Blobs records (documents, messages, notifications, favorites, leads) still need organization_id backfilled once real organizations exist -- tracked in MIGRATION_PLAN.md, not yet scheduled
No migration-runner tool is set up yet -- the schema has been extended twice in-place (safe only because it's never been run); a real runner is needed before a second migration file is ever created
F057's verifier/logger is ready but nothing calls it yet -- no payment webhook infrastructure exists at all
F051's cross-org query has no pagination -- fine at zero real data, will need LIMIT/cursor handling before real staff-scale data exists (SYS-API-005)

Customer documents/images are stored as base64 data URIs in Blobs; new care_hub_documents/file_assets Postgres tables use an opaque storageRef instead, but the actual object-storage provider is still undecided
Two independent, near-duplicated single-file staff/customer console apps (admin.html, myaccount.html) -- still not extracted
F-numbering collision between audit findings (F001-F036) and Business Care Hub functions (F001-F060) -- mitigated via naming convention
src/settings/blobsSettingsStore.js remains Blobs-backed (F056 stayed on Blobs by design)
F026/F027/F028/F049/F050/F052's logic remains engine-only, waiting on pricing/plan-limit owner decisions -- now the clear next milestone

CSV export (src/export/csvExport.js, F053) has no formula-injection guard for spreadsheet software -- open finding from Session 9's security review, and F053 still has no persistence layer of its own (it's a pure formatter over already-queried data, which is correct -- not a gap)
releaseRecommendation: NOT READY, but the persistence layer is now essentially complete for everything that doesn't require an owner decision about business content. 34 functions have real tested logic, 21 of them with real (unit-tested, not-yet-live) Postgres persistence, 335 passing tests. Two things unlock the next phase of real progress, in priority order: (1) provision a live Neon database and verify the entire src/db/ layer against it -- currently zero of 99 persistence tests have touched a real database, and this gap should close before more code is added on top of it; (2) the pricing decision (OWNER_DECISIONS.md #2), which unblocks F026/F027/F028/F050/F052 -- five fully-engineered functions waiting only on real numbers.
